author: @chrisparsonson420
text: Do we really need all those gears (I'm not thinking of people racing). I think back to the days of the three speed. Which never broke and which was more than enough in most circumstances

author: @rawandrealserendipity
text: Brilliant Luke. 🥳 I didn’t know that’s how they worked. Thanks for sharing.

author: @Teknopottu
text: Great presentation! Could be very usefull for DIYers looking to take advantage of this type of gearing and for the rest, it's a thing you know more now!

author: @totherarf
text:
I remember .... back (just after wheels were made round) there were no derailleur gears!
No. Seriously!
They had a big hub with 3 gears in it and an actuator that was a thin chain coming out of the middle of the axle. This connected to a cable ending in your handgrip. Twist the handgrip one way and you go up a gear. Twist it the other way and you go down. I am fairly sure it was a set of planetary gears .... but back then it was just Magic!
Then this French company started making new fangled gears like they needed for the "Tour de France" et voila! Derailleur was born!

Back then we did crazy stuff with bikes. Racing handlebars were twisted to be upside down and the more progressive (with more pocket money) went for "Cow Horns"! We were on fire! We would take an older bike and cut the forks off and elongate them with tubing to give a kind of "Chopper" ..... a bit hard to do good wheelies with but we were COOL!

Then I grew up and got a Yamaha FS1E (50cc of pure power) ...... and pedal bikes were suddenly not cool! ;0)
To be fair neather was riding 15 miles across Manchester on a rainy morning to get to college, but thats work for you! ;0)

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
I suspect that we are from the same century!!
I hope you are having a great day! 🙂❤️XxX.

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text: @AndreaDingbatt Every morning work out if you are sucking in air regularly. If the answer is yes, remind yourself it could be worse! ;o)

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text: @colinhamer6506 I can't remember taking one apart but it has to be a set of planetaries in there!

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@colinhamer6506 That's so true!!
When the "flash" gears  became a thing, they were so expensive!! 

I had a spiteful nature, ~ I had never had a New bike, let alone a bicycle with more than 3gears!! 

And so, when I cruised past the local 'racers',,,
  -   on the 3gear I still had,
 It gave me a particular kind of Joy, 😁!! 
     especially when they were struggling 🤣
I still baulk at paying for New bicycles, thriftyness is an important way of life...
😊.
I hope you are having a good weekend. XxX.

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@totherarf They were a source of bafflement to me also!
I could fix the 3gear system, but anything more complex was mysterious, I am in full agreement about the planetary system within!!

In fact, I also thought that they held captive a Gremlin, to be released, if an owner tried to tinker, or learn their ways!! 😁
I hope you are having a good weekend. XxX

author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
text: How do the click shifters work?

replies.author: @samuelthomasengardio
replies.text: On modern road bikes. I assume one click ratchets a pulley, the adversary click releases a spring loaded locking mechanism. Typical mountain bikes have locking points in between each gear on the shifter.

replies.author: @samuelthomasengardio
replies.text: Old 12 speeds were super analog and foolproof. All of this pertains to the spring loaded derailleur being regulated by cable tension at the shifter.

replies.author: @samuelthomasengardio

author: @WileHeCoyote
text: GREAT VIDEO LUKE!! BIKE SPROCKETS, CHAINS, and DERAILLEURS  where among my first loves in the world of mechanical exploration AND STILL ARE TO THIS DAY! its hard to get a more realistic visualization of the application of the relationship between rotational speed and torque with a fixed input force!

text: I’m almost as old as the mountains too but I never knew how it works.
